signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Hawkish RBA Crowding
signals.text: GBP/AUD G8 spread at 1.169% as AUD underperforms peers by -0.804% on average. Despite RBA Assistant Governor Hunter's hawkish stance and a 4.35% vs 3.75% carry advantage for AUD, the 'hawkish trade' is reported as crowded. AUD/USD testing 0.7146 as spec long positioning (74.8%) faces liquidation risk if Middle East inflation risks are already priced. GBP remains the G8 leader (+0.365%) supported by a 5.05% UK 10Y yield.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + PPI Surprise
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (long% 22.3%) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias and Q1 PPI input surge of 1.4%. With retail 76% long, a sentiment-positioning clash is emerging. If the RBNZ signals a hike on 27 May to combat building inflation pressures, the crowded spec short is vulnerable to a squeeze. NZD/USD at 0.586.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.94 remains supported by a +282bp SOFR-TONA carry differential, yet the options market signals acute caution. 25d RR at -1.66 (strong put skew) indicates institutional demand for JPY strength protection despite the carry bid. 160.00 remains the critical level for BoJ verbal or physical intervention. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 17.8 (normal volatility).

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 21.6% vs HV30 at 10.9% creates a massive +10.7% VRP, pricing extreme protection relative to realized movement. Retail sentiment is 71% short, diverging from the COT LF net long of +55,794. This institutional-retail gap suggests retail is fading the RBA's hiking cycle, increasing the risk of retail capitulation if AUD/USD holds above 0.7120.
